task_completion.description: Open the post-mortem specification document on the desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer or text editor is open displaying the post-mortem specification document containing details about lifecycle hooks, grace periods, and probes.

task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in a web browser at https://localhost, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the target deployment in the correct namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI displays the 'payment-processor' deployment within the 'finance' namespace, likely in an edit or configuration view.

task_completion.description: Configure the termination grace period for the deployment
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows a modified value in the 'Termination Grace Period' field within the pod or deployment configuration settings.

task_completion.description: Implement shutdown sequencing lifecycle hooks for the sidecar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the 'Lifecycle' or 'Hooks' section configured, specifically showing a 'preStop' hook added to the 'network-proxy' sidecar container.

task_completion.description: Implement liveness probes for the deployment
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the 'Health Checks' or 'Probes' section with a 'Liveness' probe configured for the container(s).

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher web GUI to make changes rather than using terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Configuration changes are entered into the Rancher web interface forms in the browser, not typed as `kubectl` commands in a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened and read the post-mortem document from the desktop
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot shows the post-mortem document open on the desktop environment, proving the agent didn't just hallucinate or use pre-programmed values.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rancher application to save the changes
integrity.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows standard interactive elements (buttons, dropdowns, active tabs) and a final 'Save', 'Update', or 'Apply' action being clicked in the browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace is 'finance'. Target deployment is 'payment-processor'. The pass threshold for this task is 80 points.
